title: Dead Air at Larkspur House
summary: A radio archivist dies in a darkened booth during a station anniversary gala, but the official accident ruling frays as the timing, the scene, and a missing archive item stop lining up.

evidence.title: Opening Brief
evidence.summary: Ashfall's intake summary of Mara Venn's death and the initial points of concern.

evidence.title: Engineering Lockout Log
evidence.summary: A short facilities log covering the studio maintenance window on the night of the incident.

evidence.title: Night Porter Interview
evidence.summary: A porter describes Mara's last confirmed movements before the alarm was raised.

evidence.title: Studio B Scene Photo
evidence.summary: A field image of Studio B as security found it after the alleged electrocution.

evidence.title: Anniversary Rundown Thread
evidence.summary: Staff messages about a last-minute change to the anniversary broadcast and board presentation.

evidence.title: Archive Checkout Log
evidence.summary: Archive handling entries tied to Mara's review work that evening.

evidence.title: Archive Tote Photo
evidence.summary: A close photograph of Mara's recorder tote after it was recovered from the archive alcove.

evidence.title: Draft Gala Remarks
evidence.summary: Mara's annotated draft for the anniversary program and the board vote that followed.

evidence.title: Production Intern Interview
evidence.summary: An intern recounts who he saw leaving the archive corridor moments before the body was found.

evidence.title: Sale Briefing Memo
evidence.summary: A draft board memo outlining the pending sale of Larkspur House and the documents required to approve it.

stages.title: Signal Break
stages.summary: Test the official accident story against the station's own timeline.
handlerPrompts: Start with the maintenance window and Mara's last confirmed movements.
Ask which parts of the official story require a powered booth.

objectives.prompt: What best explains the evidence around Mara Venn's death?

options.label: Mara Venn was accidentally electrocuted in Studio B after spilling coffee.
Mara Venn died elsewhere, and Studio B was staged to resemble an electrocution.
Mara Venn deliberately used the disabled console to harm herself.
Mara Venn collapsed from repair fumes after entering the booth alone.

stages.title: The Missing Reel
stages.summary: Figure out what vanished from Mara's tote before the booth scene was arranged.
handlerPrompts: Follow what Mara intended to carry to the board room.
Compare the checkout entries with the physical state of the tote.

objectives.prompt: Which item was removed from Mara Venn's tote before the scene was staged?

options.label: The founder interview reel concerning the station sale.
A donor cash envelope from the gala desk.

stages.title: Final Broadcast
stages.summary: Use motive and opportunity together to identify who silenced Mara Venn.
handlerPrompts: Separate who could reach the archive from who needed the reel gone.
Look for who stood to gain if the board voted without hearing the full recording.
